videos.title: EUA SEM DINHEIRO? — TRUMP QUER QUE ÁRABES PAGUEM A GUERRA
videos.description:
Os Estados Unidos dão sinais de desgaste na guerra, enquanto Donald Trump avalia pressionar países árabes a pagar pelo conflito com o Irã. Ao mesmo tempo, a Espanha desafia os EUA e expõe divisões dentro da OTAN. O cenário muda — e pode escalar ainda mais.

CUPOM DE DESCONTO: CAFEDIALETICO
https://veroo.com.br/

FONTES:

https://www.aljazeera.com/news/livebl...

https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cwy...

videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã

videos.title: Guerra do Irã AO VIVO | “RISCO DE GUERRA NUCLEAR É REAL” | Trump intensifica ataque | Coronel Mac...
videos.description:
#Irã #Trump #Israel

Barkha Dutt entra AO VIVO com o renomado analista militar Douglas Macgregor enquanto Donald Trump se dirige à nação, prometendo intensificar os ataques dos EUA contra o Irã nas próximas 2 a 3 semanas para "terminar o trabalho". O anúncio fez com que os preços globais do petróleo disparassem, decepcionando os mercados que esperavam uma redução da escalada na guerra com o Irã.

Macgregor classifica o conflito como "insano" e afirma que o discurso de Trump ficou muito aquém das expectativas. Ele argumenta que o envolvimento dos EUA está intimamente ligado aos objetivos estratégicos de Israel, alertando que o risco de uma guerra regional mais ampla está crescendo, incluindo o perigo real de uma escalada nuclear.

Em uma forte mensagem para a Índia, Macgregor insta Narendra Modi a intervir diplomaticamente e dialogar com Trump, alertando que as consequências econômicas, especialmente o aumento dos preços dos combustíveis, podem em breve afetar as famílias indianas.

A conversa também examina a possibilidade de uma operação terrestre limitada dos EUA no Irã, destacando os sérios riscos que ela representa para as tropas americanas, a estabilidade do Oriente Médio e a economia global.

And again, I'm not impinging Islamabad. I'm not saying they are not sincere. What I'm saying is that Prime Minister Modi is much better positioned to do that. You have a lot at stake in in this war and this war is is coming to your kitchens. It's going to affect your farmers. it's going to affect your your lifestyle, your standard of living. So, I don't think this is the time to wish everybody well and stand on the sidelines. I think roll up your sleeves and get involved with this because if you don't, this this train has left the station and it's the train to hell and we don't all need to ride it. Absolutely. I I know that Trump and PM Modi have spoken uh but I think India has stayed out publicly and I think what I'm hearing you say Colonel is that it's time to actually get involved to bring the principles to some sort of agreement here. Just a last question. I know you've got a heart out in a minute. You know, I spent about 11 days in the Gulf uh trying to get the perspective from there and I was able to go right up to the edge of the straight of Hormuz from the Gulf of Oman. And I met a mariner and he told me and he told me this 5 days ago. He said, "If this war doesn't end in a week, it's going to push the global economy back by at least four to five years. What is your biggest fear right now? And if you could advise Trump, if you met Trump, let's say you just walked into Donald Trump and you had to say one thing to him for the sake of America, for the sake of the world, what would that be? Uh, end the conflict immediately, suspend all further operations, announce publicly that in the interest of humanity and and the the world, uh, the bombing is going to stop. We're going to end the conflict right here and now. We want to sit down and talk with the leadership of Iran and hammer out some mutually acceptable agreement. And if they don't want to speak to us, that's where Prime Minister Modi comes in. Then they can talk to others. But we're stopping. Now, the problem for President Trump, though, is Israel. Israel wants the destruction of Iran and the destruction of the rest of the world. it seems in the process. If it if it serves their purposes, I suspect that's true. Well, Colonel Douglas McGregor, I've got to thank you for never pulling your punches. Uh I know you're so widely followed here in India. I've got like people are just in the audience stream saying thank you for getting him. He was absolutely the best voice to hear right after that extremely disappointing address to the nation by Donald Trump. We thank you for your time. We're going to let you go because you're much in demand, but we hope to reconnect with you again very soon. Uh, I would have hoped in better times, but it looks like this war isn't ending anytime soon. Thank you very much, sir. Take care. Thank you as well. My best to everybody. Thank you. Bye-bye. And we leave our audience now with the key points of Donald Trump's speech where he said, "Finish the job and put a two to three week timeline on this war ending." How he came up with this figure only he knows. Take a look. Sir, please finish the job. Every one of them. and we are going to finish the job and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very close. I want to thank our allies shortly. We are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. It's great to see you here. Thank you for watching our work. If you haven't subscribed yet, don't forget to click the bell icon and subscribe to Mojo Story and support independent robust journalists.

videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

00:00 – Discurso de Trump sobre a guerra com o Irã

06:41 – Usinas de dessalinização do Irã
08:03 – Riscos para os países do Golfo
10:41 – Aumento da presença militar dos EUA no Golfo
14:50 – Riscos das operações terrestres no Irã
18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã
20:30 – Trump e a OTAN
22:29 – O papel do Paquistão nas negociações de paz

videos.title: Guerra do Irã AO VIVO | “RISCO DE GUERRA NUCLEAR É REAL” | Trump intensifica ataque | Coronel Mac...
videos.description:
#Irã #Trump #Israel

Barkha Dutt entra AO VIVO com o renomado analista militar Douglas Macgregor enquanto Donald Trump se dirige à nação, prometendo intensificar os ataques dos EUA contra o Irã nas próximas 2 a 3 semanas para "terminar o trabalho". O anúncio fez com que os preços globais do petróleo disparassem, decepcionando os mercados que esperavam uma redução da escalada na guerra com o Irã.

Macgregor classifica o conflito como "insano" e afirma que o discurso de Trump ficou muito aquém das expectativas. Ele argumenta que o envolvimento dos EUA está intimamente ligado aos objetivos estratégicos de Israel, alertando que o risco de uma guerra regional mais ampla está crescendo, incluindo o perigo real de uma escalada nuclear.

Em uma forte mensagem para a Índia, Macgregor insta Narendra Modi a intervir diplomaticamente e dialogar com Trump, alertando que as consequências econômicas, especialmente o aumento dos preços dos combustíveis, podem em breve afetar as famílias indianas.

A conversa também examina a possibilidade de uma operação terrestre limitada dos EUA no Irã, destacando os sérios riscos que ela representa para as tropas americanas, a estabilidade do Oriente Médio e a economia global.

By the way, I'm sure he's talked to the Iranians and it's quite clear to me that the Iranians want good relations with you and are going to work to get the tankers through the straight of war moves. Uh, so I think that's all good, but I think uh, Prime Minister Modi should make the effort to contact President Trump directly because Prime Minister Modi has good relations with Israel. If the if he is is in a position to influence things, the Israelis are going to be much more comfortable with whatever agreement he comes up with or he helps to negotiate than they will be with Islamabad. And again, I'm not impinging Islamabad. I'm not saying they are not sincere. What I'm saying is that Prime Minister Modi is much better positioned to do that. Now, I don't know what's actually going on. I don't know if your foreign minister has spoken with Secretary Rubio or with President Trump. I hope he has because India really is a great power. You have a lot at stake in in this war and this war is is coming to your kitchens. It's going to affect your farmers. it's going to affect your your lifestyle, your standard of living. So, I don't think this is the time to wish everybody well and stand on the sidelines. I think roll up your sleeves and get involved with this because if you don't, this this train has left the station and it's the train to hell and we don't all need to ride it. Absolutely. I I know that Trump and PM Modi have spoken uh but I think India has stayed out publicly and I think what I'm hearing you say Colonel is that it's time to actually get involved to bring the principles to some sort of agreement here. Just a last question. I know you've got a heart out in a minute. You know, I spent about 11 days in the Gulf uh trying to get the perspective from there and I was able to go right up to the edge of the straight of Hormuz from the Gulf of Oman. And I met a mariner and he told me and he told me this 5 days ago. He said, "If this war doesn't end in a week, it's going to push the global economy back by at least four to five years. What is your biggest fear right now? And if you could advise Trump, if you met Trump, let's say you just walked into Donald Trump and you had to say one thing to him for the sake of America, for the sake of the world, what would that be? Uh, end the conflict immediately, suspend all further operations, announce publicly that in the interest of humanity and and the the world, uh, the bombing is going to stop. We're going to end the conflict right here and now. We want to sit down and talk with the leadership of Iran and hammer out some mutually acceptable agreement. And if they don't want to speak to us, that's where Prime Minister Modi comes in. Then they can talk to others. But we're stopping. Now, the problem for President Trump, though, is Israel. Israel wants the destruction of Iran and the destruction of the rest of the world. it seems in the process. If it if it serves their purposes, I suspect that's true. Well, Colonel Douglas McGregor, I've got to thank you for never pulling your punches. Uh I know you're so widely followed here in India. I've got like people are just in the audience stream saying thank you for getting him. He was absolutely the best voice to hear right after that extremely disappointing address to the nation by Donald Trump. We thank you for your time. We're going to let you go because you're much in demand, but we hope to reconnect with you again very soon. Uh, I would have hoped in better times, but it looks like this war isn't ending anytime soon. Thank you very much, sir. Take care. Thank you as well. My best to everybody. Thank you. Bye-bye. And we leave our audience now with the key points of Donald Trump's speech where he said, "Finish the job and put a two to three week timeline on this war ending." How he came up with this figure only he knows. Take a look. Sir, please finish the job. Every one of them. and we are going to finish the job and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very close. I want to thank our allies shortly. We are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. It's great to see you here. Thank you for watching our work. If you haven't subscribed yet, don't forget to click the bell icon and subscribe to Mojo Story and support independent robust journalists.

videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã

videos.title: Guerra do Irã AO VIVO | “RISCO DE GUERRA NUCLEAR É REAL” | Trump intensifica ataque | Coronel Mac...
videos.description:
#Irã #Trump #Israel

Barkha Dutt entra AO VIVO com o renomado analista militar Douglas Macgregor enquanto Donald Trump se dirige à nação, prometendo intensificar os ataques dos EUA contra o Irã nas próximas 2 a 3 semanas para "terminar o trabalho". O anúncio fez com que os preços globais do petróleo disparassem, decepcionando os mercados que esperavam uma redução da escalada na guerra com o Irã.

Macgregor classifica o conflito como "insano" e afirma que o discurso de Trump ficou muito aquém das expectativas. Ele argumenta que o envolvimento dos EUA está intimamente ligado aos objetivos estratégicos de Israel, alertando que o risco de uma guerra regional mais ampla está crescendo, incluindo o perigo real de uma escalada nuclear.

Em uma forte mensagem para a Índia, Macgregor insta Narendra Modi a intervir diplomaticamente e dialogar com Trump, alertando que as consequências econômicas, especialmente o aumento dos preços dos combustíveis, podem em breve afetar as famílias indianas.

A conversa também examina a possibilidade de uma operação terrestre limitada dos EUA no Irã, destacando os sérios riscos que ela representa para as tropas americanas, a estabilidade do Oriente Médio e a economia global.

He's obviously or or his foreign minister has talked directly. Your foreign minister is exceptionally gifted and brilliant man. By the way, I'm sure he's talked to the Iranians and it's quite clear to me that the Iranians want good relations with you and are going to work to get the tankers through the straight of war moves. Uh, so I think that's all good, but I think uh, Prime Minister Modi should make the effort to contact President Trump directly because Prime Minister Modi has good relations with Israel. If the if he is is in a position to influence things, the Israelis are going to be much more comfortable with whatever agreement he comes up with or he helps to negotiate than they will be with Islamabad. And again, I'm not impinging Islamabad. I'm not saying they are not sincere. What I'm saying is that Prime Minister Modi is much better positioned to do that. Now, I don't know what's actually going on. I don't know if your foreign minister has spoken with Secretary Rubio or with President Trump. I hope he has because India really is a great power. You have a lot at stake in in this war and this war is is coming to your kitchens. It's going to affect your farmers. it's going to affect your your lifestyle, your standard of living. So, I don't think this is the time to wish everybody well and stand on the sidelines. I think roll up your sleeves and get involved with this because if you don't, this this train has left the station and it's the train to hell and we don't all need to ride it. Absolutely. I I know that Trump and PM Modi have spoken uh but I think India has stayed out publicly and I think what I'm hearing you say Colonel is that it's time to actually get involved to bring the principles to some sort of agreement here. Just a last question. I know you've got a heart out in a minute. You know, I spent about 11 days in the Gulf uh trying to get the perspective from there and I was able to go right up to the edge of the straight of Hormuz from the Gulf of Oman. And I met a mariner and he told me and he told me this 5 days ago. He said, "If this war doesn't end in a week, it's going to push the global economy back by at least four to five years. What is your biggest fear right now? And if you could advise Trump, if you met Trump, let's say you just walked into Donald Trump and you had to say one thing to him for the sake of America, for the sake of the world, what would that be? Uh, end the conflict immediately, suspend all further operations, announce publicly that in the interest of humanity and and the the world, uh, the bombing is going to stop. We're going to end the conflict right here and now. We want to sit down and talk with the leadership of Iran and hammer out some mutually acceptable agreement. And if they don't want to speak to us, that's where Prime Minister Modi comes in. Then they can talk to others. But we're stopping. Now, the problem for President Trump, though, is Israel. Israel wants the destruction of Iran and the destruction of the rest of the world. it seems in the process. If it if it serves their purposes, I suspect that's true. Well, Colonel Douglas McGregor, I've got to thank you for never pulling your punches. Uh I know you're so widely followed here in India. I've got like people are just in the audience stream saying thank you for getting him. He was absolutely the best voice to hear right after that extremely disappointing address to the nation by Donald Trump. We thank you for your time. We're going to let you go because you're much in demand, but we hope to reconnect with you again very soon. Uh, I would have hoped in better times, but it looks like this war isn't ending anytime soon. Thank you very much, sir. Take care. Thank you as well. My best to everybody. Thank you. Bye-bye. And we leave our audience now with the key points of Donald Trump's speech where he said, "Finish the job and put a two to three week timeline on this war ending." How he came up with this figure only he knows. Take a look. Sir, please finish the job. Every one of them. and we are going to finish the job and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very close. I want to thank our allies shortly. We are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. It's great to see you here. Thank you for watching our work. videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

00:00 – Discurso de Trump sobre a guerra com o Irã

06:41 – Usinas de dessalinização do Irã
08:03 – Riscos para os países do Golfo
10:41 – Aumento da presença militar dos EUA no Golfo
14:50 – Riscos das operações terrestres no Irã
18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã
20:30 – Trump e a OTAN
22:29 – O papel do Paquistão nas negociações de paz

videos.title: Guerra do Irã AO VIVO | “RISCO DE GUERRA NUCLEAR É REAL” | Trump intensifica ataque | Coronel Mac...
videos.description:
#Irã #Trump #Israel

Barkha Dutt entra AO VIVO com o renomado analista militar Douglas Macgregor enquanto Donald Trump se dirige à nação, prometendo intensificar os ataques dos EUA contra o Irã nas próximas 2 a 3 semanas para "terminar o trabalho". O anúncio fez com que os preços globais do petróleo disparassem, decepcionando os mercados que esperavam uma redução da escalada na guerra com o Irã.

Macgregor classifica o conflito como "insano" e afirma que o discurso de Trump ficou muito aquém das expectativas. Ele argumenta que o envolvimento dos EUA está intimamente ligado aos objetivos estratégicos de Israel, alertando que o risco de uma guerra regional mais ampla está crescendo, incluindo o perigo real de uma escalada nuclear.

Em uma forte mensagem para a Índia, Macgregor insta Narendra Modi a intervir diplomaticamente e dialogar com Trump, alertando que as consequências econômicas, especialmente o aumento dos preços dos combustíveis, podem em breve afetar as famílias indianas.

A conversa também examina a possibilidade de uma operação terrestre limitada dos EUA no Irã, destacando os sérios riscos que ela representa para as tropas americanas, a estabilidade do Oriente Médio e a economia global.

Uh yeah, I I think uh India should get off the sidelines. Now you have what 9.2 9.3 million of your citizens that have been working in the Persian Gulf region. I don't know if they've been evacuated or not. I'm sure some have left and I'm sure and I'm sure Prime Minister Modi is very concerned about that which is appropriate. He's obviously or or his foreign minister has talked directly. Your foreign minister is exceptionally gifted and brilliant man. By the way, I'm sure he's talked to the Iranians and it's quite clear to me that the Iranians want good relations with you and are going to work to get the tankers through the straight of war moves. Uh, so I think that's all good, but I think uh, Prime Minister Modi should make the effort to contact President Trump directly because Prime Minister Modi has good relations with Israel. If the if he is is in a position to influence things, the Israelis are going to be much more comfortable with whatever agreement he comes up with or he helps to negotiate than they will be with Islamabad. And again, I'm not impinging Islamabad. I'm not saying they are not sincere. What I'm saying is that Prime Minister Modi is much better positioned to do that. Now, I don't know what's actually going on. I don't know if your foreign minister has spoken with Secretary Rubio or with President Trump. I hope he has because India really is a great power. You have a lot at stake in in this war and this war is is coming to your kitchens. It's going to affect your farmers. it's going to affect your your lifestyle, your standard of living. So, I don't think this is the time to wish everybody well and stand on the sidelines. I think roll up your sleeves and get involved with this because if you don't, this this train has left the station and it's the train to hell and we don't all need to ride it. Absolutely. I I know that Trump and PM Modi have spoken uh but I think India has stayed out publicly and I think what I'm hearing you say Colonel is that it's time to actually get involved to bring the principles to some sort of agreement here. Just a last question. I know you've got a heart out in a minute. You know, I spent about 11 days in the Gulf uh trying to get the perspective from there and I was able to go right up to the edge of the straight of Hormuz from the Gulf of Oman. And I met a mariner and he told me and he told me this 5 days ago. He said, "If this war doesn't end in a week, it's going to push the global economy back by at least four to five years. What is your biggest fear right now? And if you could advise Trump, if you met Trump, let's say you just walked into Donald Trump and you had to say one thing to him for the sake of America, for the sake of the world, what would that be? Uh, end the conflict immediately, suspend all further operations, announce publicly that in the interest of humanity and and the the world, uh, the bombing is going to stop. We're going to end the conflict right here and now. We want to sit down and talk with the leadership of Iran and hammer out some mutually acceptable agreement. And if they don't want to speak to us, that's where Prime Minister Modi comes in. Then they can talk to others. But we're stopping. Now, the problem for President Trump, though, is Israel. Israel wants the destruction of Iran and the destruction of the rest of the world. it seems in the process. If it if it serves their purposes, I suspect that's true. Well, Colonel Douglas McGregor, I've got to thank you for never pulling your punches. Uh I know you're so widely followed here in India. I've got like people are just in the audience stream saying thank you for getting him. He was absolutely the best voice to hear right after that extremely disappointing address to the nation by Donald Trump. We thank you for your time. We're going to let you go because you're much in demand, but we hope to reconnect with you again very soon. Uh, I would have hoped in better times, but it looks like this war isn't ending anytime soon. Thank you very much, sir. Take care. Thank you as well. My best to everybody. Thank you. Bye-bye. And we leave our audience now with the key points of Donald Trump's speech where he said, "Finish the job and put a two to three week timeline on this war ending." How he came up with this figure only he knows. Take a look. Sir, please finish the job. Every one of them. and we are going to finish the job and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very close. I want to thank our allies shortly. We are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. It's great to see you here. Thank you for watching our work. If you haven't subscribed yet, don't forget to click the bell icon and subscribe to Mojo Story and support independent robust journalists.

videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

00:00 – Discurso de Trump sobre a guerra com o Irã

10:41 – Aumento da presença militar dos EUA no Golfo
14:50 – Riscos das operações terrestres no Irã
18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã
20:30 – Trump e a OTAN

videos.title: Guerra do Irã AO VIVO | “RISCO DE GUERRA NUCLEAR É REAL” | Trump intensifica ataque | Coronel Mac...
videos.description:
#Irã #Trump #Israel

Barkha Dutt entra AO VIVO com o renomado analista militar Douglas Macgregor enquanto Donald Trump se dirige à nação, prometendo intensificar os ataques dos EUA contra o Irã nas próximas 2 a 3 semanas para "terminar o trabalho". O anúncio fez com que os preços globais do petróleo disparassem, decepcionando os mercados que esperavam uma redução da escalada na guerra com o Irã.

Macgregor classifica o conflito como "insano" e afirma que o discurso de Trump ficou muito aquém das expectativas. Ele argumenta que o envolvimento dos EUA está intimamente ligado aos objetivos estratégicos de Israel, alertando que o risco de uma guerra regional mais ampla está crescendo, incluindo o perigo real de uma escalada nuclear.

Em uma forte mensagem para a Índia, Macgregor insta Narendra Modi a intervir diplomaticamente e dialogar com Trump, alertando que as consequências econômicas, especialmente o aumento dos preços dos combustíveis, podem em breve afetar as famílias indianas.

A conversa também examina a possibilidade de uma operação terrestre limitada dos EUA no Irã, destacando os sérios riscos que ela representa para as tropas americanas, a estabilidade do Oriente Médio e a economia global.

I I know that Trump and PM Modi have spoken uh but I think India has stayed out publicly and I think what I'm hearing you say Colonel is that it's time to actually get involved to bring the principles to some sort of agreement here. Just a last question. I know you've got a heart out in a minute. You know, I spent about 11 days in the Gulf uh trying to get the perspective from there and I was able to go right up to the edge of the straight of Hormuz from the Gulf of Oman. And I met a mariner and he told me and he told me this 5 days ago. He said, "If this war doesn't end in a week, it's going to push the global economy back by at least four to five years. Well, Colonel Douglas McGregor, I've got to thank you for never pulling your punches. Uh I know you're so widely followed here in India. We're going to let you go because you're much in demand, but we hope to reconnect with you again very soon. Uh, I would have hoped in better times, but it looks like this war isn't ending anytime soon. Thank you very much, sir. Take care. Thank you as well. My best to everybody. Thank you. Bye-bye. And we leave our audience now with the key points of Donald Trump's speech where he said, "Finish the job and put a two to three week timeline on this war ending." How he came up with this figure only he knows. Take a look. Sir, please finish the job. Every one of them. and we are going to finish the job and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very close. I want to thank our allies shortly. We are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. It's great to see you here. Thank you for watching our work. videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

00:00 – Discurso de Trump sobre a guerra com o Irã
05:23 – Ameaça de atingir o Irã “com extrema força”
06:41 – Usinas de dessalinização do Irã
08:03 – Riscos para os países do Golfo
10:41 – Aumento da presença militar dos EUA no Golfo
14:50 – Riscos das operações terrestres no Irã
18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã
20:30 – Trump e a OTAN
22:29 – O papel do Paquistão nas negociações de paz

videos.title: Guerra do Irã AO VIVO | “RISCO DE GUERRA NUCLEAR É REAL” | Trump intensifica ataque | Coronel Mac...
videos.description:
#Irã #Trump #Israel

Barkha Dutt entra AO VIVO com o renomado analista militar Douglas Macgregor enquanto Donald Trump se dirige à nação, prometendo intensificar os ataques dos EUA contra o Irã nas próximas 2 a 3 semanas para "terminar o trabalho". O anúncio fez com que os preços globais do petróleo disparassem, decepcionando os mercados que esperavam uma redução da escalada na guerra com o Irã.

Macgregor classifica o conflito como "insano" e afirma que o discurso de Trump ficou muito aquém das expectativas. Ele argumenta que o envolvimento dos EUA está intimamente ligado aos objetivos estratégicos de Israel, alertando que o risco de uma guerra regional mais ampla está crescendo, incluindo o perigo real de uma escalada nuclear.

Em uma forte mensagem para a Índia, Macgregor insta Narendra Modi a intervir diplomaticamente e dialogar com Trump, alertando que as consequências econômicas, especialmente o aumento dos preços dos combustíveis, podem em breve afetar as famílias indianas.

A conversa também examina a possibilidade de uma operação terrestre limitada dos EUA no Irã, destacando os sérios riscos que ela representa para as tropas americanas, a estabilidade do Oriente Médio e a economia global.

I also know that anything that we sent to the Pakistanis that were that was supposed to be transferred to Iran has elicited the same response from Iran over and over and over again. We're not negotiating and I don't think they will because the strategic initiative is in their hands. Yeah. Yeah. Do you see a role for India? Would you would you India has kept quiet and focused on getting our own people our ships out. Do look at Go ahead sir. Just wanted to point out that Hexith in the meanwhile has a tweet out on X called back to the stone age. I just don't understand this way of talking but anyway. Yeah, it's it's horrendous. Uh yeah, I I think uh India should get off the sidelines. Now you have what 9.2 9.3 million of your citizens that have been working in the Persian Gulf region. I don't know if they've been evacuated or not. I'm sure some have left and I'm sure and I'm sure Prime Minister Modi is very concerned about that which is appropriate. He's obviously or or his foreign minister has talked directly. Your foreign minister is exceptionally gifted and brilliant man. By the way, I'm sure he's talked to the Iranians and it's quite clear to me that the Iranians want good relations with you and are going to work to get the tankers through the straight of war moves. Uh, so I think that's all good, but I think uh, Prime Minister Modi should make the effort to contact President Trump directly because Prime Minister Modi has good relations with Israel. If the if he is is in a position to influence things, the Israelis are going to be much more comfortable with whatever agreement he comes up with or he helps to negotiate than they will be with Islamabad. And again, I'm not impinging Islamabad. I'm not saying they are not sincere. What I'm saying is that Prime Minister Modi is much better positioned to do that. Now, I don't know what's actually going on. I don't know if your foreign minister has spoken with Secretary Rubio or with President Trump. I hope he has because India really is a great power. You have a lot at stake in in this war and this war is is coming to your kitchens. It's going to affect your farmers. it's going to affect your your lifestyle, your standard of living. So, I don't think this is the time to wish everybody well and stand on the sidelines. I think roll up your sleeves and get involved with this because if you don't, this this train has left the station and it's the train to hell and we don't all need to ride it. Absolutely. I I know that Trump and PM Modi have spoken uh but I think India has stayed out publicly and I think what I'm hearing you say Colonel is that it's time to actually get involved to bring the principles to some sort of agreement here. Just a last question. I know you've got a heart out in a minute. You know, I spent about 11 days in the Gulf uh trying to get the perspective from there and I was able to go right up to the edge of the straight of Hormuz from the Gulf of Oman. And I met a mariner and he told me and he told me this 5 days ago. He said, "If this war doesn't end in a week, it's going to push the global economy back by at least four to five years. What is your biggest fear right now? And if you could advise Trump, if you met Trump, let's say you just walked into Donald Trump and you had to say one thing to him for the sake of America, for the sake of the world, what would that be? Uh, end the conflict immediately, suspend all further operations, announce publicly that in the interest of humanity and and the the world, uh, the bombing is going to stop. We're going to end the conflict right here and now. We want to sit down and talk with the leadership of Iran and hammer out some mutually acceptable agreement. And if they don't want to speak to us, that's where Prime Minister Modi comes in. Then they can talk to others. But we're stopping. Now, the problem for President Trump, though, is Israel. Israel wants the destruction of Iran and the destruction of the rest of the world. it seems in the process. If it if it serves their purposes, I suspect that's true. Well, Colonel Douglas McGregor, I've got to thank you for never pulling your punches. Uh I know you're so widely followed here in India. I've got like people are just in the audience stream saying thank you for getting him. He was absolutely the best voice to hear right after that extremely disappointing address to the nation by Donald Trump. We thank you for your time. We're going to let you go because you're much in demand, but we hope to reconnect with you again very soon. Uh, I would have hoped in better times, but it looks like this war isn't ending anytime soon. Thank you very much, sir. Take care. Thank you as well. My best to everybody. Thank you. Bye-bye. And we leave our audience now with the key points of Donald Trump's speech where he said, "Finish the job and put a two to three week timeline on this war ending." How he came up with this figure only he knows. Take a look. Sir, please finish the job. Every one of them. and we are going to finish the job and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very close. I want to thank our allies shortly. We are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. It's great to see you here. Thank you for watching our work. videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

00:00 – Discurso de Trump sobre a guerra com o Irã
05:23 – Ameaça de atingir o Irã “com extrema força”
06:41 – Usinas de dessalinização do Irã
08:03 – Riscos para os países do Golfo
10:41 – Aumento da presença militar dos EUA no Golfo
14:50 – Riscos das operações terrestres no Irã
18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã
20:30 – Trump e a OTAN
22:29 – O papel do Paquistão nas negociações de paz

videos.title: Guerra do Irã AO VIVO | “RISCO DE GUERRA NUCLEAR É REAL” | Trump intensifica ataque | Coronel Mac...
videos.description:
#Irã #Trump #Israel

Barkha Dutt entra AO VIVO com o renomado analista militar Douglas Macgregor enquanto Donald Trump se dirige à nação, prometendo intensificar os ataques dos EUA contra o Irã nas próximas 2 a 3 semanas para "terminar o trabalho". O anúncio fez com que os preços globais do petróleo disparassem, decepcionando os mercados que esperavam uma redução da escalada na guerra com o Irã.

Macgregor classifica o conflito como "insano" e afirma que o discurso de Trump ficou muito aquém das expectativas. Ele argumenta que o envolvimento dos EUA está intimamente ligado aos objetivos estratégicos de Israel, alertando que o risco de uma guerra regional mais ampla está crescendo, incluindo o perigo real de uma escalada nuclear.

Em uma forte mensagem para a Índia, Macgregor insta Narendra Modi a intervir diplomaticamente e dialogar com Trump, alertando que as consequências econômicas, especialmente o aumento dos preços dos combustíveis, podem em breve afetar as famílias indianas.

A conversa também examina a possibilidade de uma operação terrestre limitada dos EUA no Irã, destacando os sérios riscos que ela representa para as tropas americanas, a estabilidade do Oriente Médio e a economia global.

The Pakistanis have a very close relationship with the Turks, and the Turks have always known that if they needed a nuclear weapon, they could get it from Pakistan. Second thing is it was really the Chinese that offered something and I read through the points that the Chinese offered. They were not unreasonable because their focus was to get to the peace the negotiating table and to stop the killing, stop the war. very similar to what Trump has tried to do, he says he's tried to do with Ukraine. And I think it's unfortunate that it has not it has not precipitated anything. As far as I know, there's been no formal response. I also know that anything that we sent to the Pakistanis that were that was supposed to be transferred to Iran has elicited the same response from Iran over and over and over again. We're not negotiating and I don't think they will because the strategic initiative is in their hands. Yeah. Yeah. Do you see a role for India? Just wanted to point out that Hexith in the meanwhile has a tweet out on X called back to the stone age. I just don't understand this way of talking but anyway. Yeah, it's it's horrendous. I don't know if they've been evacuated or not. I'm sure some have left and I'm sure and I'm sure Prime Minister Modi is very concerned about that which is appropriate. So, I don't think this is the time to wish everybody well and stand on the sidelines. I think roll up your sleeves and get involved with this because if you don't, this this train has left the station and it's the train to hell and we don't all need to ride it. Absolutely. I I know that Trump and PM Modi have spoken uh but I think India has stayed out publicly and I think what I'm hearing you say Colonel is that it's time to actually get involved to bring the principles to some sort of agreement here. Just a last question. I know you've got a heart out in a minute. You know, I spent about 11 days in the Gulf uh trying to get the perspective from there and I was able to go right up to the edge of the straight of Hormuz from the Gulf of Oman. And I met a mariner and he told me and he told me this 5 days ago. He said, "If this war doesn't end in a week, it's going to push the global economy back by at least four to five years. And if you could advise Trump, if you met Trump, let's say you just walked into Donald Trump and you had to say one thing to him for the sake of America, for the sake of the world, what would that be? We're going to end the conflict right here and now. We want to sit down and talk with the leadership of Iran and hammer out some mutually acceptable agreement. And if they don't want to speak to us, that's where Prime Minister Modi comes in. Then they can talk to others. But we're stopping. Now, the problem for President Trump, though, is Israel. Israel wants the destruction of Iran and the destruction of the rest of the world. it seems in the process. If it if it serves their purposes, I suspect that's true. Well, Colonel Douglas McGregor, I've got to thank you for never pulling your punches. Uh I know you're so widely followed here in India. I've got like people are just in the audience stream saying thank you for getting him. He was absolutely the best voice to hear right after that extremely disappointing address to the nation by Donald Trump. We thank you for your time. We're going to let you go because you're much in demand, but we hope to reconnect with you again very soon. Uh, I would have hoped in better times, but it looks like this war isn't ending anytime soon. Thank you very much, sir. Take care. Thank you as well. My best to everybody. Thank you. Bye-bye. And we leave our audience now with the key points of Donald Trump's speech where he said, "Finish the job and put a two to three week timeline on this war ending." How he came up with this figure only he knows. Take a look. Sir, please finish the job. Every one of them. and we are going to finish the job and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very close. I want to thank our allies shortly. We are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. It's great to see you here. Thank you for watching our work. videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

00:00 – Discurso de Trump sobre a guerra com o Irã
05:23 – Ameaça de atingir o Irã “com extrema força”
06:41 – Usinas de dessalinização do Irã
08:03 – Riscos para os países do Golfo
10:41 – Aumento da presença militar dos EUA no Golfo
14:50 – Riscos das operações terrestres no Irã
18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã
20:30 – Trump e a OTAN
22:29 – O papel do Paquistão nas negociações de paz

videos.title: Guerra do Irã AO VIVO | “RISCO DE GUERRA NUCLEAR É REAL” | Trump intensifica ataque | Coronel Mac...
videos.description:
#Irã #Trump #Israel

Barkha Dutt entra AO VIVO com o renomado analista militar Douglas Macgregor enquanto Donald Trump se dirige à nação, prometendo intensificar os ataques dos EUA contra o Irã nas próximas 2 a 3 semanas para "terminar o trabalho". O anúncio fez com que os preços globais do petróleo disparassem, decepcionando os mercados que esperavam uma redução da escalada na guerra com o Irã.

Macgregor classifica o conflito como "insano" e afirma que o discurso de Trump ficou muito aquém das expectativas. Ele argumenta que o envolvimento dos EUA está intimamente ligado aos objetivos estratégicos de Israel, alertando que o risco de uma guerra regional mais ampla está crescendo, incluindo o perigo real de uma escalada nuclear.

Em uma forte mensagem para a Índia, Macgregor insta Narendra Modi a intervir diplomaticamente e dialogar com Trump, alertando que as consequências econômicas, especialmente o aumento dos preços dos combustíveis, podem em breve afetar as famílias indianas.

A conversa também examina a possibilidade de uma operação terrestre limitada dos EUA no Irã, destacando os sérios riscos que ela representa para as tropas americanas, a estabilidade do Oriente Médio e a economia global.

So on the basis of that alone, I'm not sure that that's the ideal platform to come to any agreement that includes Israel. So that's the first thing. Second thing is it was really the Chinese that offered something and I read through the points that the Chinese offered. They were not unreasonable because their focus was to get to the peace the negotiating table and to stop the killing, stop the war. very similar to what Trump has tried to do, he says he's tried to do with Ukraine. And I think it's unfortunate that it has not it has not precipitated anything. As far as I know, there's been no formal response. I also know that anything that we sent to the Pakistanis that were that was supposed to be transferred to Iran has elicited the same response from Iran over and over and over again. We're not negotiating and I don't think they will because the strategic initiative is in their hands. Yeah. Yeah. Do you see a role for India? Would you would you India has kept quiet and focused on getting our own people our ships out. Do look at Go ahead sir. Just wanted to point out that Hexith in the meanwhile has a tweet out on X called back to the stone age. I just don't understand this way of talking but anyway. Yeah, it's it's horrendous. Uh yeah, I I think uh India should get off the sidelines. Now you have what 9.2 9.3 million of your citizens that have been working in the Persian Gulf region. I don't know if they've been evacuated or not. I'm sure some have left and I'm sure and I'm sure Prime Minister Modi is very concerned about that which is appropriate. He's obviously or or his foreign minister has talked directly. Your foreign minister is exceptionally gifted and brilliant man. By the way, I'm sure he's talked to the Iranians and it's quite clear to me that the Iranians want good relations with you and are going to work to get the tankers through the straight of war moves. Uh, so I think that's all good, but I think uh, Prime Minister Modi should make the effort to contact President Trump directly because Prime Minister Modi has good relations with Israel. If the if he is is in a position to influence things, the Israelis are going to be much more comfortable with whatever agreement he comes up with or he helps to negotiate than they will be with Islamabad. And again, I'm not impinging Islamabad. I'm not saying they are not sincere. What I'm saying is that Prime Minister Modi is much better positioned to do that. Now, I don't know what's actually going on. I don't know if your foreign minister has spoken with Secretary Rubio or with President Trump. I hope he has because India really is a great power. You have a lot at stake in in this war and this war is is coming to your kitchens. It's going to affect your farmers. it's going to affect your your lifestyle, your standard of living. So, I don't think this is the time to wish everybody well and stand on the sidelines. I think roll up your sleeves and get involved with this because if you don't, this this train has left the station and it's the train to hell and we don't all need to ride it. Absolutely. I I know that Trump and PM Modi have spoken uh but I think India has stayed out publicly and I think what I'm hearing you say Colonel is that it's time to actually get involved to bring the principles to some sort of agreement here. Just a last question. I know you've got a heart out in a minute. You know, I spent about 11 days in the Gulf uh trying to get the perspective from there and I was able to go right up to the edge of the straight of Hormuz from the Gulf of Oman. And I met a mariner and he told me and he told me this 5 days ago. He said, "If this war doesn't end in a week, it's going to push the global economy back by at least four to five years. What is your biggest fear right now? And if you could advise Trump, if you met Trump, let's say you just walked into Donald Trump and you had to say one thing to him for the sake of America, for the sake of the world, what would that be? Uh, end the conflict immediately, suspend all further operations, announce publicly that in the interest of humanity and and the the world, uh, the bombing is going to stop. We're going to end the conflict right here and now. We want to sit down and talk with the leadership of Iran and hammer out some mutually acceptable agreement. And if they don't want to speak to us, that's where Prime Minister Modi comes in. Then they can talk to others. But we're stopping. Now, the problem for President Trump, though, is Israel. Israel wants the destruction of Iran and the destruction of the rest of the world. it seems in the process. If it if it serves their purposes, I suspect that's true. Well, Colonel Douglas McGregor, I've got to thank you for never pulling your punches. Uh I know you're so widely followed here in India. I've got like people are just in the audience stream saying thank you for getting him. He was absolutely the best voice to hear right after that extremely disappointing address to the nation by Donald Trump. We thank you for your time. We're going to let you go because you're much in demand, but we hope to reconnect with you again very soon. Uh, I would have hoped in better times, but it looks like this war isn't ending anytime soon. Thank you very much, sir. Take care. Thank you as well. My best to everybody. Thank you. Bye-bye. And we leave our audience now with the key points of Donald Trump's speech where he said, "Finish the job and put a two to three week timeline on this war ending." How he came up with this figure only he knows. Take a look. Sir, please finish the job. Every one of them. and we are going to finish the job and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very close. I want to thank our allies shortly. We are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. It's great to see you here. Thank you for watching our work. videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

00:00 – Discurso de Trump sobre a guerra com o Irã
05:23 – Ameaça de atingir o Irã “com extrema força”
06:41 – Usinas de dessalinização do Irã
08:03 – Riscos para os países do Golfo
10:41 – Aumento da presença militar dos EUA no Golfo
14:50 – Riscos das operações terrestres no Irã
18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã
20:30 – Trump e a OTAN
22:29 – O papel do Paquistão nas negociações de paz

videos.title: Guerra do Irã AO VIVO | “RISCO DE GUERRA NUCLEAR É REAL” | Trump intensifica ataque | Coronel Mac...
videos.description:
#Irã #Trump #Israel

Barkha Dutt entra AO VIVO com o renomado analista militar Douglas Macgregor enquanto Donald Trump se dirige à nação, prometendo intensificar os ataques dos EUA contra o Irã nas próximas 2 a 3 semanas para "terminar o trabalho". O anúncio fez com que os preços globais do petróleo disparassem, decepcionando os mercados que esperavam uma redução da escalada na guerra com o Irã.

Macgregor classifica o conflito como "insano" e afirma que o discurso de Trump ficou muito aquém das expectativas. Ele argumenta que o envolvimento dos EUA está intimamente ligado aos objetivos estratégicos de Israel, alertando que o risco de uma guerra regional mais ampla está crescendo, incluindo o perigo real de uma escalada nuclear.

Em uma forte mensagem para a Índia, Macgregor insta Narendra Modi a intervir diplomaticamente e dialogar com Trump, alertando que as consequências econômicas, especialmente o aumento dos preços dos combustíveis, podem em breve afetar as famílias indianas.

A conversa também examina a possibilidade de uma operação terrestre limitada dos EUA no Irã, destacando os sérios riscos que ela representa para as tropas americanas, a estabilidade do Oriente Médio e a economia global.

I mean, remember, Pakistan has promised to nuke Israel if it uses a nuclear weapon. They said, "Well, we'll we'll respond immediately." The Pakistanis have a very close relationship with the Turks, and the Turks have always known that if they needed a nuclear weapon, they could get it from Pakistan. So on the basis of that alone, I'm not sure that that's the ideal platform to come to any agreement that includes Israel. So that's the first thing. Second thing is it was really the Chinese that offered something and I read through the points that the Chinese offered. They were not unreasonable because their focus was to get to the peace the negotiating table and to stop the killing, stop the war. very similar to what Trump has tried to do, he says he's tried to do with Ukraine. And I think it's unfortunate that it has not it has not precipitated anything. As far as I know, there's been no formal response. I also know that anything that we sent to the Pakistanis that were that was supposed to be transferred to Iran has elicited the same response from Iran over and over and over again. We're not negotiating and I don't think they will because the strategic initiative is in their hands. Yeah. Yeah. Do you see a role for India? Would you would you India has kept quiet and focused on getting our own people our ships out. Do look at Go ahead sir. Just wanted to point out that Hexith in the meanwhile has a tweet out on X called back to the stone age. I just don't understand this way of talking but anyway. Yeah, it's it's horrendous. Uh yeah, I I think uh India should get off the sidelines. Now you have what 9.2 9.3 million of your citizens that have been working in the Persian Gulf region. I don't know if they've been evacuated or not. I'm sure some have left and I'm sure and I'm sure Prime Minister Modi is very concerned about that which is appropriate. He's obviously or or his foreign minister has talked directly. Your foreign minister is exceptionally gifted and brilliant man. By the way, I'm sure he's talked to the Iranians and it's quite clear to me that the Iranians want good relations with you and are going to work to get the tankers through the straight of war moves. Uh, so I think that's all good, but I think uh, Prime Minister Modi should make the effort to contact President Trump directly because Prime Minister Modi has good relations with Israel. If the if he is is in a position to influence things, the Israelis are going to be much more comfortable with whatever agreement he comes up with or he helps to negotiate than they will be with Islamabad. And again, I'm not impinging Islamabad. I'm not saying they are not sincere. What I'm saying is that Prime Minister Modi is much better positioned to do that. Now, I don't know what's actually going on. I don't know if your foreign minister has spoken with Secretary Rubio or with President Trump. I hope he has because India really is a great power. You have a lot at stake in in this war and this war is is coming to your kitchens. It's going to affect your farmers. it's going to affect your your lifestyle, your standard of living. So, I don't think this is the time to wish everybody well and stand on the sidelines. I think roll up your sleeves and get involved with this because if you don't, this this train has left the station and it's the train to hell and we don't all need to ride it. Absolutely. I I know that Trump and PM Modi have spoken uh but I think India has stayed out publicly and I think what I'm hearing you say Colonel is that it's time to actually get involved to bring the principles to some sort of agreement here. Just a last question. I know you've got a heart out in a minute. You know, I spent about 11 days in the Gulf uh trying to get the perspective from there and I was able to go right up to the edge of the straight of Hormuz from the Gulf of Oman. And I met a mariner and he told me and he told me this 5 days ago. He said, "If this war doesn't end in a week, it's going to push the global economy back by at least four to five years. What is your biggest fear right now? And if you could advise Trump, if you met Trump, let's say you just walked into Donald Trump and you had to say one thing to him for the sake of America, for the sake of the world, what would that be? Uh, end the conflict immediately, suspend all further operations, announce publicly that in the interest of humanity and and the the world, uh, the bombing is going to stop. We're going to end the conflict right here and now. We want to sit down and talk with the leadership of Iran and hammer out some mutually acceptable agreement. And if they don't want to speak to us, that's where Prime Minister Modi comes in. Then they can talk to others. But we're stopping. Now, the problem for President Trump, though, is Israel. Israel wants the destruction of Iran and the destruction of the rest of the world. it seems in the process. If it if it serves their purposes, I suspect that's true. Well, Colonel Douglas McGregor, I've got to thank you for never pulling your punches. Uh I know you're so widely followed here in India. I've got like people are just in the audience stream saying thank you for getting him. He was absolutely the best voice to hear right after that extremely disappointing address to the nation by Donald Trump. We thank you for your time. We're going to let you go because you're much in demand, but we hope to reconnect with you again very soon. Uh, I would have hoped in better times, but it looks like this war isn't ending anytime soon. Thank you very much, sir. Take care. Thank you as well. My best to everybody. Thank you. Bye-bye. And we leave our audience now with the key points of Donald Trump's speech where he said, "Finish the job and put a two to three week timeline on this war ending." How he came up with this figure only he knows. Take a look. Sir, please finish the job. Every one of them. and we are going to finish the job and we're going to finish it very fast. We're getting very close. I want to thank our allies shortly. We are going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the stone ages where they belong. It's great to see you here. Thank you for watching our work. videos.title: "Catástrofe total": Coronel Macgregor critica duramente Trump em reação impulsiva ao discurso sob...
videos.description:
O Coronel Douglas Macgregor (aposentado) é um consultor de risco geopolítico com décadas de experiência em defesa e política externa dos EUA.

Nesta edição do ANI Broadcast com Ishaan Prakash, o Coronel Macgregor apresenta sua primeira reação ao discurso de Trump sobre o Irã, chamando-o de "teatro do absurdo" e alertando para uma catástrofe iminente. Ele afirma que Trump "perdeu o rumo" e que a guerra vai piorar.

#ANIBroadcast #IshaanPrakash #Macgregor #DonaldTrump #Irã #Hormuz #PetróleoBruto #Netanyahu #Israel #Golfo #IdadeDaPedra

Marcadores de tempo

00:00 – Discurso de Trump sobre a guerra com o Irã
05:23 – Ameaça de atingir o Irã “com extrema força”
06:41 – Usinas de dessalinização do Irã
08:03 – Riscos para os países do Golfo
10:41 – Aumento da presença militar dos EUA no Golfo
14:50 – Riscos das operações terrestres no Irã
18:13 – Negociações de paz EUA-Irã
20:30 – Trump e a OTAN
22:29 – O papel do Paquistão nas negociações de paz

videos.title: Douglas Macgregor: A Guerra do Irã Destruiu a OTAN, os Estados do Golfo, Israel e o Império Ameri...
videos.description:
Douglas Macgregor é um coronel aposentado, veterano de combate e ex-conselheiro sênior do Secretário de Defesa dos EUA. O Coronel Macgregor argumenta que a Guerra do Irã contribuiu para a destruição da OTAN, dos Estados do Golfo, de Israel e do Império Americano. (Gravado em 1º de abril de 2026)

videos.transcript: Pesquisar transcrição Welcome back. We are joined today by Colonel Douglas McGregor, a decorated combat veteran, author, and a former adviser to the US Secretary of Defense. Uh, as always, thank you very much for taking the time. Oh, happy to be here. So, we're getting some mixed messages from President Trump. That is the That's a That's a big change. Well, no. Yeah, good point. But well, we heard here that the that the US already won the war. Uh but if uh the Iranians don't agree to a ceasefire, they will destroy all of the uh infrastructure in Iran if they don't open the straight of Hormuz. At the same time, he also argues that the US is ready to leave and the straight of Hermoose should be the problem of the Europeans and the regional states. So um h how are you assessing the state in this war? because uh I guess you know I understand you know there's a lot of different audiences to communicate to and um you know one wants to put pressure while at the same time have something offered on the table but how do you make sense of the current position now? Well I'm not sure a decision has been made uh or if it has he's not sharing it. I think it's a very strange set of circumstances. I mean, you have the claim, this incessant drum beat, we've already won. We destroyed everything that could be destroyed. Uh, they're down to nothing. They can't keep up. They're running out of missiles. Uh, their back is against the wall, etc., etc., etc. Well, if that's true, why is it that every time he makes some sort of statement along these lines, a wave of missile attacks comes out of Iran? uh tactical theater ballistic missiles, drones that do enormous damage to Israel and to frankly the Gulf Emirates. I mean, if that's really the case, why hasn't Iran already hoisted the the white flag? I mean, I I just don't I think that those claims have to be dismissed up front. What I am hearing from people who are on the inside that I trust is that we have perhaps reduced uh Iran's uh ballistic missile capability from 100% down to perhaps 70%. Uh that their air defense is being very effective against the unmanned surveillance aircraft selectively against other kinds of aircraft but has not performed as well as they'd hoped. But they have always been willing to absorb the strikes. They've always known that their infrastructure would be brutalized and they think the the payoff is is worth the trouble. So that's the first thing. Second thing is if things are going so well and we've had so much success with the air and missile campaign, why do we want to introduce ground troops into the equation? Doesn't seem to make a lot of sense. So I don't think you're getting a truthful assessment from the US side. I think the president is desperate. I think he faces public humiliation inside the United States as well as overseas if he admits the truth. And when he starts talking about, well, maybe I'll just leave and dump all of this into the laps of Europeans, we ought to stop and and consider a few things. First of all, commercial traffic through the straight of Hormuz has fallen by about 95 to 97%. uh 15 million barrels of oil a day have been taken offline. The physical shortages have already hit so hard that right now Singapore jet fuel is selling at 231 dollars a barrel. And as long as the war persists, the real people who shut down the straight of Hormuz, who are not Iranians, they're sitting in London. They're called Lloyds of London. The insurance brokers aren't going to ensure anything that goes through the straight right now because it's a war zone. So if you really want to open up the straight, you've got to stop the war. Uh simply passing an ultimatum against to the Iranians and then turning to the Europeans who were really never consulted on this whole business. Neither were the Japanese. Neither were the South Koreans. I mean, frankly, we ignored all of our allies and simply acted independently in a way that we decided to act and it was never really in our interest to do so. When you turn to a friendly country country like India, you discover the Indians are apppoplelectic. Well, they do $180 billion of business and trade a year with the Emirates. Suddenly that's gone. And if we continue to destroy infrastructure in Iran, the the Iranians have said, "Fine, if you hit our deselination plants, if you hit our oil infrastructure, we're going to turn around and obliterate everything in the Persian Gulf." So what does he plan to do? He wants to land troops on some islands presumably. Right now the United States Navy sails 500 to 800 miles away from the Gulf and stays on the move cutting giant squares in the ocean because it doesn't want to be sunk. It doesn't want its ships on the bottom. Uh how do you get the troops in there? Can you get close enough without being detected and without coming on fire to sort of put the troops in a position from which they can attack something? Then how do you propose to attack wherever you want to attack? Can you account for all the potential air defense systems that might be there, including shoulder fired missiles? uh can you ensure that you've neutralized this brilliant network of intelligence surveillance reconnaissance platforms with uh strike systems that are all over Iran and they're miles deep inside the country anywhere from 10 miles to 200 miles to 300 miles. You know, all of these questions are never really answered. It's the big handwave. Oh, we're successful. We're brilliant. We've done everything right. Uh, these pesky Iranians are too dumb to surrender, but we think we've done enough damage. Now, from whom have we not heard? Who is really in charge? Israel. Why are we in this war? Because we suddenly decided that Iran was this satanic evil element in the international system that had to be purged for anyone anywhere in civilization to be safe. No, that's a lot of nonsense. We're there because Israel wants Iran taken off the board, which is the the phrase used by the neocons in Washington. This whole thing was sold from the very beginning on the on the assumption that first of all, this will be a short campaign. Uh we would be surprised if they didn't fall apart in 96 hours. That's what I was told. Secondly, uh once we've taken Iran off the board, Israel is safe. It has no worries. It can bully uh the region. It can push its power and influence to the limits. And oh, don't worry about the Turks. We'll keep them under control. Uh we're close with the Turks. Turks don't want trouble with us. They'll do what we say. So, the attitude in Israel was fine. Then we just need to obliterate Iran. And oh, by the way, if we do this right, we and you can move in and take control of the oil fields. Never mind Iraq. Don't mind Syria. Don't mind Turkey. Well, the Emirates have discovered that depending on us is not a very good idea. And I think President Trump realizes he's got to make a decision. He said, ' Can we do everything? Probably not. Then what can we do? How much longer can we keep this up in the Persian Gulf? Everybody forgets we're visiting. We're not a resident. We're very dependent on other people's harbors, other people's air bases. How much longer can we do this? We haven't even talked about the space-based intelligence that's been on uh loan to the uh Iranians. So, he's got to answer this question. And I think tonight he he recognizes he's got to go one way or the other. He's got to say, "It's over. We've won. We're leaving." In which case, Israel is in trouble because Israel hasn't won. and Israel must have us backing them unconditionally to win anything. So I don't think the Israelis will approve of that. And it's very hard for me to believe that the immensely powerful scionist billionaires in the United States who are real really the foundation for the Israel lobby's influence and power are prepared to throw Israel under the bus as they say. So, I I I find that hard to imagine. And perhaps President Trump will say, "Well, we're going to continue to support Israel." Well, that's like saying, uh, you know, I have all these dead cats, but I'm going to continue feeding them as long as possible because that's what Israel looks like right now. It's in ruins. It's not getting any better. We don't know how many people have died, but certainly a lot more than the Israelis were prepared to accept. I think that's pretty obvious. Uh, so what do the Iranians want? Well, the Iranians want us out of the Persian Gulf. What have we done for decades? We've surrounded Iran, placing bases wherever we could from which we could threaten Iran. The Iranians have said, "No thanks. Not anymore." The Sykes Pico states that were created at Versailles in the aftermath of World War I, gone. We don't accept that anymore. So, what do they want besides us out of the Gulf? They're not going to come to peace terms with the Israelis as long as the Israelis are expanding into Lebanon and killing people there by the thousands or expanding into Syria. So, how do we deliver on Israel? Are we really in control? I don't think we are. Yeah. No, I spoke to a to an Iranian as well who who made a point that if they go up the escalation ladder, they can do a lot of different things. Uh some of most of them I would have predicted, but one of them I thought was interesting that is uh to reward Iraq for its assistance. They could also change the borders of the region since you you know mentioned the Sykes Pico that is uh the Kuwait could go be joining Iraq essentially. So there there's a lot of um surprises which seems to be outside our control at the moment in terms of how the region will be impacted by this. Uh what I wanted to ask as well though was how do you see the region beyond um uh the Middle East being affected? That is uh especially the NATO relationship. I find this to be interesting that is uh for on one hand Trump has been arguing that NATO is now a paper tiger. Marco Rubio said perhaps we should just uh you know we have to reconsider this relationship. They're only there when we need to help them not the other way around. And I kind of can understand from both sides that is from the European perspective the Americans never told them uh didn't inform them never cared and then now they want them to go on the suicide mission into the straight of moose. On the other side the Europeans blocked the access to military bases airspace. Uh Poland was asked to hand over some patriot batteries to help out the United States in the region and they declined. So, I kind of can understand where the mutual frustration comes from. But what what do you think this war though will do to the transatlantic partnership? Uh several years ago in 1991 during the air campaign uh while most of us that were prepared to invade Iraq were sitting on the sidelines watching the air campaign unfold in front of us. We were literally literally up on the Saudi border watching all of the sorties every night that went in with bombers and various kinds of aircraft. And one night uh this was very early in the morning actually. This would have been about 4 in the morning. Uh I was trying to get some rest and I was suddenly awakened by a soldier. He said, "Sir, quickly cut get to the CP. We need you right now." I said, "Okay, what's happening?" He said, "Well, we have a report that we're under fire." I said, "Really?" Thought that was interesting. I didn't think the Iraqis during the air campaign would risk an attack, but all things are possible. Well, this man on the phone was very uh we we have airto ground bombs uh falling on our location. Well, I've never heard of anything other than airto ground bombs. Let's all get under the artillery fire with ghost troop." In [laughter] other words, if everybody up there is under fire, why are we going up to join them? Why don't we do something intelligent and try to find out where it's coming from and whatever. Anybody who thinks that American soldiers are stupid is wrong. They're actually very intelligent people. So, finally, I got the executive officer on the phone and he said, "Look, I'm sorry about this. This man had been watching flares that were dropped by A10s and others. And ever since the Soviet state system collapsed, we've been trying to repurpose and repurpose the famous statement, if NATO is not out of area, well then it's out of business. We don't need it. And I told everybody in NATO, get smart. Get out. You don't want to go to all of the hell holes that we have identified for invasion and occupation. That's all." Which means even though the numbers of tankers used to average 75 a day going through the straight, now it's 3 to four. And the Americans are saying that is unacceptable. You can't talk to those people. If you do that, you're going back to Munich. You're negotiating with Hitler. Yes. Here you go again. So, we're in a bit of a pickle. Uh, and I think the Iranians hold the strategic initiative. They are not at the end of their rope. I think he's got to make a decision. Do I bail on this? It's a stinker. I made a mistake. find a way to get out and blame it on other people, which is the standard Trump approach, or do I say, "No, I'm going to obliterate you. You know, you refuse to negotiate with me, the great marketer, the great salesman, then I'm going to wage a warning air and missile attack, and I'm going to bring in ground troops, and I'm going to cut you off for the Gulf and force the Gulf open." So I think the Iranians hold the strategic initiative and we stand an excellent chance of ending up like a a giant 14 or 15 or 16 or 18 wheel truck headed down the side of a mountain for a concrete wall. we'll just end up battering ourselves against a concrete wall one more time.
